summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a smoking early‑1970 set highlighted by The Other One, Black Peter and Dancing in the Streets.
review: The recording is praised for its clear, improved sound quality, delivering an energetic performance that many fans rank among their favorites. Standout moments include a fiery version of The Other One, a vocal‑driven Black Peter, and a jubilant Dancing In The Streets/Midnight Hour close. Overall the show exudes the confidence and jam‑heavy spirit that defined 1970.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are strongly positive (average 4.6 stars), highlighting both superb musical moments and a high‑quality soundboard source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note the copy sounds much better than other versions, describing the sound as clear and well‑balanced with no reported flaws.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as high‑energy and jam‑rich, capturing the confident early‑1970 vibe that made that year legendary.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his vocal delivery on Black Peter and his playing on The Other One.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mention of Tom Constanten’s impending departure adds a unique keyboard flavor to the night.
